title: Rhamphicarpa fistulosa
taxonomy: Rhamphicarpa fistulosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a holoparasitic plant, meaning it obtains its nutrients by connecting to the vascular tissue of the roots of a host plant. This plant is native to tropical Africa and is known for its unique parasitic relationship with its host. Rhamphicarpa fistulosa is often found in areas with dense vegetation, where it can easily connect to the roots of other plants. The classification of Rhamphicarpa fistulosa is further supported by its classification as a member of the APG III system, which places it in the order Lamiales.
